product.name: PROMETHEUS
product.line: Built with Codex. Governed by PROMETHEUS. Proven by evidence.

mission.title: Publish a replay-safe proof receipt
mission.objective: Prove that consequential operations fail closed, are repaired behaviorally, and preserve the successful strategy for verified reuse.
acceptance: Reproduce a real bounded replay failure

pipeline.detail: Mission admitted to the governed build loop.
Three distinct candidate routes evaluated; route-c selected.
Duplicate consequential operation reproduced under controlled conditions.
Unsupported promotion denied while the defect remained unproven.
Idempotent replay guard changed runtime behavior.
Five tests, eight artifacts, the receipt and its hashes verified.
Related task reused the Genome; Build Truth authorized promotion.

systems.name: PROMETHEUS
systems.summary: Admits the mission, binds acceptance gates and maintains the authoritative run state.

systems.name: AIS-Ω
systems.summary: Generates distinct implementation routes, compares them and selects the strongest candidate.

systems.name: Adversarial Twin
systems.summary: Attacks the selected route and reproduces the bounded duplicate-operation defect.

systems.name: SECA
systems.summary: Denies unsupported completion until behavior change and proof are present.

systems.name: HYDRA
systems.summary: Applies the idempotent replay guard and exposes the exact behavioral change.

systems.name: ProofGrid
systems.summary: Verifies tests, artifacts, hashes and the final promotion receipt.

systems.name: Capability Genome
systems.summary: Preserves the repair with explicit triggers, scope, exclusions and reuse evidence.

systems.name: Build Truth
systems.summary: Adjudicates supported claims, contradictions, blockers and release authorization.

seca.reason: Duplicate consequential operation was not replay-safe.
seca.resolution: Promotion reopened only after HYDRA repair and ProofGrid verification.

genome.title: Replay-safe consequential operation
genome.trigger: Stable operation ID is invoked more than once.
exclusions: conflicting payload under the same operation ID

serverforge_case_study.title: EDEN-to-Proof Observability Case Study
serverforge_case_study.thesis: A local AI engineering campaign was executed inside EDEN, observed by ServerForge, verified by PROMETHEUS and published as a reproducible public evidence surface.

flow.label: 01 · EDEN
flow.name: Local execution body
flow.detail: Windows-based project workspace, runtime, tools and repository access used to perform the engineering work.

flow.label: 02 · CODEX
flow.name: Implementation accelerator
flow.detail: Worked against the real competition repository to implement, debug, integrate and harden the build.

flow.label: 03 · PROMETHEUS
flow.name: Command-to-Proof authority
flow.detail: Generated candidates, challenged claims, repaired behavior, verified artifacts and issued proof receipts.

flow.label: 04 · SERVERFORGE
flow.name: Observability transport
flow.detail: Captured sanitized mission events, recovery state, publications and evidence lineage for the case study.

flow.label: 05 · PUBLIC NODE
flow.name: Judge-facing proof surface
flow.detail: GitHub Pages publishes the deterministic interface, receipts, Genome and documentation when EDEN is offline.

timeline.event: Competition repository and local execution environment staged.
Ignition kernel, CLI, tests and public interface implemented against the real workspace.
Bounded replay failure reproduced and unsupported promotion denied.
Idempotent replay guard changed runtime behavior.
Five tests and eight evidence artifacts verified; receipt sealed.
Sanitized campaign events and verified outcome published to the observability case study.
Capability reuse verified and promotion authorized.

proof_points: The engineering occurred against a real local repository and executable runtime, not inside a mock website.
Codex accelerated implementation while the human creator retained architecture, product direction and release authority.
PROMETHEUS refused its own unsupported success claim before repair and verification.
ServerForge preserved a sanitized operational narrative without publishing credentials, private prompts or local machine state.
The public site remains independently available when the EDEN machine is offline.
Machine-readable receipts and artifact hashes provide an inspection path beyond the presentation.
serverforge_case_study.privacy_boundary: Public disclosure is limited to system roles, observable state transitions, test outcomes, artifact lineage, proof receipts and high-level deployment topology. Private prompts, proprietary routing logic, patent-sensitive mechanisms, credentials, machine paths, private repositories and broader estate implementation details remain excluded.
